summary_one_line: A coherent XML body long enough that the parser buffers more than INPUT_CHUNK bytes ahead of the cursor, taking the grow-skip true branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An input that drives xmlCurrentChar/parser deep into a coherent XML document so that xmlBufUse(in->buf->buffer) exceeds (indx + INPUT_CHUNK) at the grow check on line 309 (true side), i.e. enough buffered, well-formed content remains ahead of the cursor that the buffer need not be grown. cmplog's plain havoc+I2S budget reaches this; grimoire's generalization/structural budget does not.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The winning cmplog seed (458 bytes) is large and ends in a coherent XML/namespace token run ('?xmlnstxlink'), and on the W path the parser reaches xmlCurrentChar (W hits 351 vs L 0) and xmlCopyCharMultiByte (W 309 vs L 0) and takes the line-309 true side (L309 W: T=6 F=1). cmplog spends its execution budget on havoc byte mutation plus efficient I2S substitution rather than on generalization re-runs, so it builds inputs that progress far enough to satisfy the buffer-ahead condition.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The grimoire (loser) seeds are short (13-52 bytes) and structurally fragmented (e.g. repeated '////////', truncated 'http://~tp://~'); they never reach xmlCurrentChar (L hits 0) and at line 309 take only the false side (L309 L: T=0 F=48). grimoire pays an extra-execution tax: the GeneralizationStage validates each <GAP> candidate by re-running, and the Grimoire structural mutators recombine generalised token/gap fragments, producing short, non-progressing inputs that keep the buffer under indx+INPUT_CHUNK so the parser falls through to the actual grow path instead of the true skip branch.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog wins because its I2SRandReplace stage plus havoc operates without grimoire's generalization + structural-recombination energy tax. The single-technique delta grimoire_structural is a net negative at this branch: grimoire's GeneralizationStage re-runs and Grimoire structural mutators spend executions on short fragmented generalised inputs, while cmplog's I2S substitution (confirmed by I2S-floor dash rows in the winner's lineage) efficiently grows coherent content that buffers more than INPUT_CHUNK ahead, taking the line-309 true branch.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is a buffer-fill comparison: take the true side when buffered bytes exceed the cursor index plus INPUT_CHUNK.
The decisive pair is cmplog beating grimoire with the single-technique delta grimoire_structural.
On the winner path the parser reaches xmlCurrentChar and xmlCopyCharMultiByte, which the loser never executes.
At the blocker line the winner takes the true side while the loser takes only the false side.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 309  T=6 F=1  T=0 F=48  if (xmlBufUse(in->buf->buffer) > (unsigned int) indx + IN...

evidence_trail.claim: The winning cmplog seed is large (458 bytes) and ends in a coherent XML namespace token run.
The loser grimoire seeds are short and structurally fragmented.
cmplog runs I2SRandReplace on top of havoc, substituting CMP operands found in the input.
grimoire adds a GeneralizationStage plus structural mutators on top of the cmplog stack, incurring extra validation re-runs.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
plus extra executions
during generalization (each candidate gap is validated by a re-run).

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 6869 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 3 --seed 9c2cb638eada1a5c` and observed 2 I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depth 11 and 12 of the 13-level ancestor chain; the trailing 'I2S-floor signal' line confirmed unwrapped I2SRandReplace contribution in the winner's lineage (lower bound).

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness in which disabling grimoire's GeneralizationStage and structural mutators (leaving plain cmplog I2S+havoc) does NOT improve reach of the buffer-ahead true branch versus full grimoire, i.e. grimoire matches or beats cmplog at this grow-skip condition.

weakest_evidence_point: Whether grimoire's loss is caused by its structural-mutation execution tax rather than by an unrelated seed-size or scheduling artifact is inferred from seed sizes and per-execution cost notes, not directly measured.
